title: ‘Not built right the first time’ — Musk’s xAI is starting over again, again
summary: Musk's xAI is revamping its AI coding tool project, starting over after previous attempts. Two new executives from Cursor have joined the effort.

title: Lawyer behind AI psychosis cases warns of mass casualty risks
summary: A lawyer warns AI chatbots are linked to mass casualty cases, outpacing safeguards. This matters because it highlights the urgent need for stricter regulations on AI technology.

title: Nyne, founded by a father-son duo, gives AI agents the human context they’re missing
summary: Nyne, a data infrastructure startup founded by a father-son duo, raised $5.3 million in seed funding. This investment will help Nyne develop AI agents with human context, addressing a key limitation in current AI technology.

title: Beyond Semantic Similarity: Introducing NVIDIA NeMo Retriever’s Generalizable Agentic Retrieval Pipeline
Steven Spielberg says he’s ‘never used AI’ in any of his films
summary: Steven Spielberg claims he's never used AI in his films, citing its incompatibility with creative writing roles. This stance highlights the ongoing debate about AI's role in the entertainment industry.

title: The biggest AI stories of the year (so far)
summary: Major AI acquisitions and indie developer successes have dominated the industry's headlines so far this year. These developments matter as they signal significant shifts in AI's growth and adoption.

title: The wild six weeks for NanoClaw’s creator that led to a deal with Docker
summary: Gavriel Cohen's open-source project NanoClaw secured a partnership with Docker in just six weeks. This rapid success matters because it highlights the potential for rapid innovation and collaboration in the open-source community.

title: Why Care About Prompt Caching in LLMs?
summary: Prompt caching in LLMs is a technique that stores frequently used input prompts to reduce call costs and latency. This matters because it can significantly improve the efficiency and scalability of large language model applications.

title: Spotify will let you edit your Taste Profile to control your recommendations
summary: Spotify introduces edit feature for Taste Profile, allowing users to control personalized playlists and recommendations. This update empowers users to refine their music experience by adjusting their profile.

title: How Vision Language Models Are Trained from “Scratch”
summary: Researchers fine-tune text-only language models to process images by adding visual inputs and training them from scratch. This allows models to learn visual representations without relying on pre-trained vision models, increasing flexibility and adaptability.

title: The $32B acquisition that one VC is calling the ‘Deal of the Decade’
summary: Google acquires cybersecurity startup Wiz for $32 billion, a record-breaking deal. This matters as it highlights the growing importance of cybersecurity in the AI and cloud era.

title: Why physical AI is becoming manufacturing’s next advantage
summary: Manufacturers are shifting from automation to physical AI, which combines robotics and AI to create more adaptable and intelligent production systems. This matters because it enables faster innovation, improved quality, and enhanced safety in the face of labor constraints and rising complexity.

title: Peacock expands into AI-driven video, mobile-first live sports, and gaming
summary: Peacock expands into AI-driven video features and mobile-first live sports and gaming. This move aims to boost growth through innovative, engaging content.

summary:
Learn how to build a powerful agentic RAG system
The post How to Build Agentic RAG with Hybrid Search appeared first on Towards Data Science.

title: Before quantum computing arrives, this startup wants enterprises already running on it
summary: Peter Sarlin launches Qutwo, a startup building infrastructure for enterprises to run on quantum computing. This infrastructure will be ready when quantum computing becomes mainstream, giving companies a head start.

title: Truecaller now lets you hang up on scammers — on behalf of your family
summary: Truecaller launched a feature allowing one family member to end scam calls on behalf of others. This feature enables real-time fraud call alerts and remote call termination.

title: DIVE: Scaling Diversity in Agentic Task Synthesis for Generalizable Tool Use
summary: Researchers introduce DIVE, a method to scale diversity in synthesized tasks for generalizable tool use in LLMs. DIVE inverts synthesis order, executing diverse tools first and deriving tasks from resulting traces. It matters because DIVE addresses the brittleness of existing methods, enabling more robust generalization.

title: A Survey of Reasoning in Autonomous Driving Systems: Open Challenges and Emerging Paradigms
summary: Researchers identify a shift in autonomous driving from perception to reasoning limitations. A systematic framework is needed to integrate large language and multimodal models into autonomous driving systems.

title: PACED: Distillation at the Frontier of Student Competence
summary: Researchers introduce PACED, a framework that optimizes large language model (LLM) distillation by focusing on the student's zone of proximal development. This approach reduces compute waste by concentrating on the student's competence frontier.

title: Measuring AI Agents' Progress on Multi-Step Cyber Attack Scenarios
summary: AI models show improved performance in multi-step cyber attack scenarios, scaling log-linearly with compute budget and outperforming predecessors. This matters as it highlights advancements in AI's autonomous cyber-attack capabilities, potentially increasing cybersecurity risks.

title: Reversible Lifelong Model Editing via Semantic Routing-Based LoRA
summary: Researchers introduce SoLA, a framework for reversible lifelong model editing in Large Language Models. SoLA uses semantic routing to dynamically activate LoRA modules, preventing semantic drift and catastrophic forgetting.

title: Build an Agent That Thinks Like a Data Scientist: How We Hit #1 on DABStep with Reusable Tool Generation
How to watch Jensen Huang’s Nvidia GTC 2026 keynote
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang will deliver the keynote address at GTC 2026, focusing on the company's vision for future computing and AI. This matters because it will reveal Nvidia's plans for innovation and leadership in the tech industry.

title: Sales automation startup Rox AI hits $1.2B valuation, sources say
summary: Rox AI, a sales automation startup, has reportedly reached a $1.2 billion valuation. The AI-native platform offers an alternative to traditional CRM tools.

title: A defense official reveals how AI chatbots could be used for targeting decisions
summary: The US military may use generative AI to rank targets and make strike recommendations, which would be reviewed by humans. This development raises concerns about the potential for AI-driven targeting decisions, highlighting the need for human oversight and accountability.

title: Facebook Marketplace now lets Meta AI respond to buyers’ messages
summary: Facebook Marketplace now offers sellers the option to use Meta AI to automatically respond to buyer inquiries. This feature saves sellers time by drafting replies based on listing information.

title: Tinder tries to lure people back to online dating with IRL events, virtual speed dating
summary: Tinder introduces in-person events and virtual speed dating to revive user base. This revamp aims to attract younger daters and reengage existing users.

title: Atlassian follows Block’s footsteps and cuts staff in the name of AI
summary: Atlassian laid off 1,600 employees (10% of its workforce) to redirect funds to AI development. This move mirrors Block's recent staff cuts, prioritizing AI investment.

title: Bumble to launch an AI dating assistant, ‘Bee’
summary: Bumble introduces AI-powered "Bee" to match users based on compatibility and goals. This new feature will shift the app's focus beyond swiping, enabling more personalized matches.

title: A writer is suing Grammarly for turning her and other authors into ‘AI editors’ without consent
summary: Journalist Julia Angwin is leading a class action lawsuit against Grammarly for allegedly using her work without consent. This matters as it raises concerns about AI-powered tools exploiting authors' work without permission.

summary:

title: Pragmatic by design: Engineering AI for the real world
summary: Product engineers increasingly integrate AI into everyday products like cars and medical devices. This matters because it enhances design, validation, and streamlining, leading to improved functionality and safety.

title: Agentic Control Center for Data Product Optimization
summary: Researchers propose a system to automate data product improvement using AI agents in a continuous optimization loop. This system enables domain experts to refine data products with human oversight, balancing automation and trust.

title: Hybrid Self-evolving Structured Memory for GUI Agents
summary: Researchers propose Hybrid Self-evolving Structured Memory (HyMEM), a graph-based memory for GUI agents that combines symbolic and continuous representations. This innovation enables GUI agents to learn from and adapt to complex workflows and interfaces.

title: HEAL: Hindsight Entropy-Assisted Learning for Reasoning Distillation
summary: Researchers propose Hindsight Entropy-Assisted Learning (HEAL), a framework to distill reasoning capabilities from large models into smaller ones. HEAL addresses the "Teacher Ceiling" limitation by actively intervening in the learning process.

title: Beyond Scalars: Evaluating and Understanding LLM Reasoning via Geometric Progress and Stability
summary: Researchers introduce TRACED, a framework to assess LLM reasoning quality through geometric kinematics, decomposing reasoning into Progress and Stability. This approach improves performance and robustness across benchmarks by identifying correct reasoning patterns.

title: Verbalizing LLM's Higher-order Uncertainty via Imprecise Probabilities
summary: Researchers propose new prompt-based uncertainty elicitation techniques for large language models using imprecise probabilities. This addresses limitations in classical probabilistic uncertainty frameworks, particularly in ambiguous question-answering and self-reflection settings.

title: How NVIDIA AI-Q Reached \#1 on DeepResearch Bench I and II
Autonomous context compression
summary:
New: Researchers added a tool to the Deep Agents SDK for autonomous context compression, allowing models to self-regulate their working memory. 
This matters because it enables more efficient and adaptive AI decision-making.

title: Rakuten fixes issues twice as fast with Codex
summary: Rakuten now uses Codex from OpenAI to automate software development, reducing mean time to repair (MTTR) by 50%. This automation enables faster and safer software shipping.

title: Hustlers are cashing in on China’s OpenClaw AI craze
summary:
New: A 27-year-old software engineer is capitalizing on China's OpenClaw AI craze by automating tasks. 
It matters because this trend highlights the rapid adoption and monetization of AI tools in China.

title: Designing AI agents to resist prompt injection
summary: Researchers develop AI defense against "prompt injection" attacks by constraining risky actions and protecting sensitive data. This matters as it prevents malicious manipulation of AI agents and safeguards user data.

title: From model to agent: Equipping the Responses API with a computer environment
summary: OpenAI has integrated a computer environment into its Responses API, allowing agents to run with files, tools, and state. This development enables secure, scalable agents, which matters for widespread AI adoption and application.

title: Wayfair boosts catalog accuracy and support speed with OpenAI
summary: Wayfair leverages OpenAI models to automate ticket triage and enhance product attributes. This boosts support speed and catalog accuracy, benefiting customers and the company's operational efficiency.

title: The Anatomy of an Agent Harness
summary: Researchers define 'harness' as systems built around AI models to turn them into work engines. This concept, called Agent Harness, consists of a model and its harness, which makes the model's intelligence useful.

title: How Coding Agents Are Reshaping Engineering, Product and Design
summary: Coding agents are being integrated into engineering, product, and design to automate software creation. This shift matters as it could significantly reduce development time and costs.

title: Building a strong data infrastructure for AI agent success
summary: Companies are rapidly adopting AI agents, with 88% using AI in at least one business function by late 2025. This surge in adoption matters because it highlights the growing need for robust data infrastructure to support the success of these AI agents.

title: How Pokémon Go is giving delivery robots an inch-perfect view of the world
summary: Pokémon Go is providing high-quality 3D mapping data to companies developing delivery robots. This data helps robots navigate complex environments with precision and accuracy.

title: Improving instruction hierarchy in frontier LLMs
summary: Researchers introduce IH-Challenge, a training method for frontier LLMs that prioritizes trusted instructions. This improves instruction hierarchy, safety, and resistance to attacks.

title: Introducing Storage Buckets on the Hugging Face Hub
Keep the Tokens Flowing: Lessons from 16 Open-Source RL Libraries
Granite 4.0 1B Speech: Compact, Multilingual, and Built for the Edge
How we built LangChain’s GTM Agent
summary: LangChain developed a GTM (Go-To-Market) agent that boosts lead conversion by 250% and saves sales reps 40 hours/month. This AI-powered agent streamlines sales processes, increasing efficiency and conversion rates.

title: How AI is turning the Iran conflict into theater
summary: AI-powered conflict tracking dashboards are being shared online, allowing users to monitor the Iran conflict in real-time. This matters because it reveals the increasing accessibility and transparency of military intelligence, potentially influencing public perception and global understanding of the conflict.

title: Ulysses Sequence Parallelism: Training with Million-Token Contexts
Evaluating Skills
summary: LangChain is building skills to integrate with coding agents like Codex and Claude Code. This effort is part of a broader industry trend to enhance AI collaboration with ecosystems and tools.

title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
summary: Railway, a cloud platform, secured $100 million in funding to develop AI-native infrastructure, challenging AWS and Google Cloud. This investment reflects growing frustration among developers with traditional cloud platforms' complexity and cost.

title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
summary: Claude Code, a terminal-based AI coding agent, now costs up to $200/month, sparking a rebellion among developers. Goose, a free open-source alternative, offers identical functionality, running locally on users' machines without subscription fees.

title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
summary: Listen Labs secured $69M in funding after a viral billboard hiring stunt that drew thousands of applicants with a coding challenge. This unconventional approach led to the hiring of several engineers and attracted major investors.

title: Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
summary: Salesforce launches a rebuilt Slackbot AI agent, capable of searching data, drafting documents, and taking action on behalf of employees. This move positions Slack at the center of the "agentic AI" movement, battling Microsoft and Google for workplace AI dominance.

title: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
summary: Anthropic launched Cowork, an AI agent that works in files without coding, built in approximately 1.5 weeks using its own Claude Code tool. This marks a major shift in AI accessibility, positioning Anthropic to compete with industry giants in conversational and productivity AI.
